title: Eudemian Ethics
subtitle: Ἠθικὰ Εὐδήμεια — The Neglected Masterpiece of Aristotelian Ethics
author: Aristotle

recommendedTranslation.title: Aristotle: The Eudemian Ethics
recommendedTranslation.note: The first complete English translation of the EE, by a leading Aristotelian scholar. Includes a lucid introduction, explanatory notes, and the three common books in their EE context.

alternativeTranslations.publisher: Cambridge University Press (Cambridge Texts in the History of Philosophy)
alternativeTranslations.note: A fluent and exact translation with helpful explanatory notes; widely praised in scholarly reviews.
The classic Loeb edition with facing Greek text; includes the Athenian Constitution and Magna Moralia.
Based on Rowe's new Oxford Classical Text (2023); the most textually authoritative translation. Includes a thorough introduction on the textual history.
A recent scholarly translation with comprehensive notes and glossary.

themes: The Role of Good Fortune in the Good Life
The Relationship Between Virtue and Natural Goods
awards: One of Aristotle's two major ethical treatises
Foundational text for the study of Aristotelian ethics alongside the Nicomachean Ethics
Rediscovered as central to understanding Aristotle's ethical development
excerpt: We are inquiring what happiness (eudaimonia) is, and what makes it attainable. Some think it is wisdom, others virtue, others pleasure. But we say that happiness is the activity of a complete life in accordance with complete virtue. And by 'complete virtue' we mean not merely virtue in the abstract, but virtue exercised, and exercised not in a single action but over a whole life.
